response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a broad background spanning web, mobile, and low-latency services, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerators. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD experience, deep neural network training or inference optimization, or hardware-software co-design work. His AI exposure is limited to application-layer agentic tooling (MCP frameworks), which is categorically different from the silicon and accelerator performance work this role demands. With a BS and roughly 17 years of experience, he meets the tenure bar, but the experience is entirely in the wrong domain.
strengths: Long tenure (~17 years) in software engineering demonstrates sustained technical growth and reliability
Demonstrated low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second, showing some performance-oriented thinking
Broad polyglot background (DLang, C/C++, TypeScript, Java) and embedded/systems exposure indicates ability to work across abstraction layers
concerns: Zero SoC/IP modeling experience — the single most critical technical requirement for this role is entirely absent from the resume
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training and inference optimization, covering all three preferred qualifications
AI involvement is confined to agentic application tooling (MCP), not hardware-level accelerator design or deep-learning workload analysis

key_factors.factor: No hardware or accelerator domain background
